claims.evidence_text: Amin and Barzegar were recognized as the sensitive (salt's tolerance threshold: 3.67 dS m −1) and tolerant (salt's tolerance threshold: 7.13 dS m −1) cultivars, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Salt-sensitive wheat cultivar Amin has a salinity tolerance threshold of 3.67 dS/m (based on relative shoot dry weight).

claims.evidence_text: Amin and Barzegar were recognized as the sensitive (salt's tolerance threshold: 3.67 dS m −1) and tolerant (salt's tolerance threshold: 7.13 dS m −1) cultivars, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Salt-tolerant wheat cultivar Barzegar has a salinity tolerance threshold of 7.13 dS/m (based on relative shoot dry weight).

claims.evidence_text: the cultivar Amin (3.67 dS m −1) is more salt-sensitive than Barzegar (7.13 dS m −1). Wheat... has exhibits moderate salinity tolerance.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat salinity tolerance threshold (EC_t) ranged 3.67-7.13 dS/m across two cultivars; wheat shows moderate salinity tolerance.

claims.evidence_text: Both wheat cultivars experienced a decrease in the investigated leaf area from the 2 dS m −1 salinity level, with the shoot and root dry weight trends remaining unchanged until the 6 dS m −1 level
claims.claim_summary: Wheat leaf area declines from 2 dS/m salinity; shoot and root dry weight trends unchanged until 6 dS/m.

claims.evidence_text: placed in a greenhouse at a temperature of 29 ± 2 °C, RH of 80%, and a natural photoperiod
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grown in greenhouse at 29 ±2 °C with 80% RH under natural photoperiod.
